home_intro: Connect a MIDI piano or play with your computer keyboard. Pick a drill below, or practice a song.
home_empty_prefix: No songs on this device yet.
home_favorites_empty: Star songs to pin them here for quick access.
songs_intro: Every song on this device. Search, filter, and star the ones you're working on.
scores_intro: Public-domain piano scores, rendered and played right in your browser.
settings_mastery_threshold: Mark a score learned at grade
play_sprint_blurb: Fresh notes every run — play as many as you can before the timer runs out.
play_daily_blurb: One minute, the same notes for everyone today. Beat your best.
play_ear_blurb: Hear a note and find it by ear. No reading required.
settings_subtitle: Preferences are saved on this device.
settings_get_help: Get help or report a bug
curriculums_intro: Your songs, grouped by curriculum. Import a curriculum pack in Settings to add a teacher's or school's collection.
tracks_intro: Follow a path downward. Everything stays unlocked — the order is a suggestion and the markers show what you've already set a score on.
progress_intro: Practice is tracked on this device — finish a run in any mode to add to it.
progress_empty: No practice logged yet — finish a run to start your streak.
import_invalid: This link does not contain a playable song.
import_added_suffix: to your library on this device.
import_source_wiki_blurb: Community-maintained scores you can open straight into Plinky.
import_intro: Plinky keeps your songs on your device rather than bundling a big library. Open a song link from one of these places — it adds the song here.
import_own_prefix: Have your own notation? Paste it on the
import_own_middle: . Got a song pack or a school's curriculum? Import it in
import_share_prefix: Want to share a song with everyone?
midi_unsupported: This browser does not expose the Web MIDI API. Use Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop or Android.
midi_unsupported_keyboard: This browser does not expose the Web MIDI API. Use Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop or Android — or play with your computer keyboard below.
play_play_highlighted: Play the highlighted note. The cursor advances when you hit the right key.
rhythm_intro: Play each note in time with the metronome at {tempo} bpm. One bar counts you in.
rhythm_dynamics: Dynamics: {label} ({evenness}% even) — a MIDI piano scores this from how evenly you strike the keys.
loop_intro: Pick a few bars and play them on repeat until they stick. Each lap is timed and scored so you can watch yourself speed up and clean up.
loop_range_hint: Looping bars {from}–{to}. Tap a note on the score to start a range, then tap another to set its end.
tempo_intro: Play at your own pace — no metronome. Plinky charts your tempo and flags where you slowed down.
tempo_steady: Nice and steady — no big slow-downs.
sprint_daily_intro: Everyone plays the same one-minute phrase today. Come back tomorrow for a new one.
sprint_intro: Fresh notes every run. Play as many correctly as you can before the timer runs out — the clock starts on your first note.
ear_intro: Listen to a note, then find it by ear. Any octave counts — you are matching the note, not the key.
ear_which_note: Which note did you hear? Play it.
timetrial_intro: Play the phrase as fast and cleanly as you can. The clock starts on your first note.
keyboard_hint_no_piano: No piano? Play with your computer keyboard:
keyboard_hint_shift: shift both hands an octave — current offset
import_no_notes: No playable notes found — is this valid ABC?
import_placeholder:
Paste ABC notation, e.g.
X:1
T:My Tune
M:4/4
L:1/4
K:C
C D E F |
backup_intro: Your songs are saved only on this device — {count} so far. Download a backup, or import a song pack (such as a curriculum shared by a teacher).
backup_import_error: That file could not be imported.
midi_debug_intro: Connect a digital piano and confirm note events arrive in the browser.
midi_debug_unsupported: This browser does not expose the Web MIDI API. Use Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop or Android — Safari (macOS and iOS) is not supported.
